name: validate
context: Lint, validate, and bundle OpenAPI YAML or JSON before generation, mocking, testing, or publishing.
info: OpenAPI validation checks that an API description is syntactically valid, structurally consistent, reference-resolvable, and aligned with local governance rules. The execution surface is Redocly CLI, which can run via `redocly` or `npx @redocly/cli@latest`, plus optional `redocly.yaml` configuration. Agents use this skill before every downstream OpenAPI action: generation, mocking, contract testing, docs publishing, or breaking-change diffing. Bundling is equally important for multi-file specs because many downstream tools behave better with a single resolved file.
triggers: check if the api spec is valid
why does codegen fail on this spec
body:
# OpenAPI Validation and Bundling

Validate the contract before using it. Most expensive OpenAPI failures come from skipping this step: generators produce broken SDKs, mocks return invalid payloads, docs omit operations, or contract tests target a schema that never actually parsed.

## Validate

```bash
nodes/openapi/scripts/validate-openapi.sh openapi.yaml
```

The script uses a local `redocly` binary if present, otherwise `npx --yes @redocly/cli@latest`. It accepts local files and remote URLs.

## Bundle Multi-file Specs

```bash
nodes/openapi/scripts/validate-openapi.sh openapi.yaml dist/openapi.bundle.yaml

# Equivalent environment-driven form
OPENAPI_BUNDLE_OUT=dist/openapi.bundle.yaml nodes/openapi/scripts/validate-openapi.sh openapi.yaml
```

Bundling rewrites a multi-file API description into one file for tools that do not reliably traverse external references.

## Redocly Configuration

Put governance rules in `redocly.yaml` and pass it through the environment:

```yaml
apis:
  main:
    root: openapi.yaml
rules:
  operation-operationId: error
  operation-summary: warn
  security-defined: error
  no-unused-components: warn
```

```bash
OPENAPI_REDOCLY_CONFIG=redocly.yaml nodes/openapi/scripts/validate-openapi.sh openapi.yaml
```

## CI Gate

```yaml
name: OpenAPI
on: [push, pull_request]
jobs:
  validate:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
      - run: nodes/openapi/scripts/validate-openapi.sh openapi.yaml dist/openapi.yaml
```

Pair this with [[openapi/diff]] for compatibility checks and [[openapi/contract-test]] for live service verification.

sections.title: Lint the contract
sections.body: Run `redocly lint openapi.yaml` or the wrapper script before generation, mocking, publishing, or merge. Linting catches invalid structure, broken references, duplicate or missing operation IDs, bad examples, undefined security, unused components, and governance rule violations.

sections.title: Bundle references
sections.body: Use `redocly bundle openapi.yaml --output dist/openapi.yaml` or `nodes/openapi/scripts/validate-openapi.sh openapi.yaml dist/openapi.yaml` when the spec uses external `$ref` files. Bundling is the safest handoff format for [[openapi/generate]], [[openapi/mock]], [[openapi/contract-test]], and documentation publishing.

sections.title: Governance rules
sections.body: A `redocly.yaml` file turns validation into repeatable API governance. Enforce operation IDs, summaries, security definitions, tag descriptions, response examples, and unused component cleanup. Keep rules strict enough to protect clients but not so strict that teams bypass the gate.

sections.title: Common failure patterns
sections.body: Typical failures are invalid YAML indentation, missing `responses`, duplicate `operationId`, path parameters not declared in `parameters`, examples that do not match schemas, undefined security schemes, and relative external refs that resolve differently in CI. Bundle and validate from the same working directory CI uses.

links.context: Fix authoring issues surfaced by validation

related.note: Validation enforces the OpenAPI object model
